description: Prebiotic chemistry experiment with three layers: d1=bond_chemistry, d2=autocatalytic_membership, d3=replication_co_occurrence. Goal: test whether structural role identity persists from mechanistic chemistry into proto-replicative organization.

hypotheses.statement: Functional Proximity Law holds in prebiotic chemistry: r(bond_chemistry, autocatalytic_membership) > r(bond_chemistry, replication_co_occurrence).
A core carbon-feedstock node remains top-tier in d1: formaldehyde (h2co) ranks within top 2 hubs in bond_chemistry.
Replicative organization centralizes around information-carrying intermediates: RNA fragments (rna_frag) ranks within top 3 hubs in replication_co_occurrence.
At least 2 molecules show strong cross-layer role divergence (rank gap >= 5) between bond_chemistry and replication_co_occurrence, indicating emergence of proto-functional specialization.

notes: X9 first-stage model-based run. If confirmed, next step is external-data replication on published prebiotic reaction-network datasets (autocatalytic set literature) with the same test structure.
